title: Simple 2x Lumber Hall Tree
summary: A minimalist hall tree built from plain 2x4/2x6 stock, with tall back legs running straight up to hook height and no back panel at all — a bench with hooks, not a bench with a decorative lattice back.
description:
The tall 72" back legs are what carry the coat hooks — they run straight up from the seat with nothing else behind them, which is what makes this hall tree read as an open, minimalist stick-frame piece rather than a solid-backed bench. Shorter front legs, side aprons, and a long front apron tie the whole frame together at seat height.

**A pair of small mitered cross supports brace the underside of the seat**, cut at matching 45° angles — this is a compact bracing detail under the seat, not a full decorative back panel like some hall tree designs use, so don't expect a large X feature on this piece. The 2x6 seat and top boards cap the frame, with a center seat support keeping the middle from flexing.

Mount six coat hooks along the tall back legs at a height that suits the household, and add cup hooks for keys or sunglasses if wanted — these are simple screw-in additions once the frame is finished and sanded.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.part: Front apron, back supports, back seat board

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 45°, long point to long point, ends perpendicular.

steps.title: Build the leg frame
steps.body: Assemble the tall back legs, short front legs, side aprons, front apron, and back supports into the main frame.

steps.title: Add the cross supports and center support
steps.body: Miter and attach the two cross supports under the seat area, then attach the center seat support.

steps.title: Attach the seat and top
steps.body: Attach the 2x6 seat and top boards to the frame.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Add the hooks
steps.body: Mount the six coat hooks along the tall back legs, and add cup hooks if wanted.

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple 2x lumber hall tree with tall back legs and coat hooks
